addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

content.content:
1	# Brain MRI Cross-Modal Retrieval Challenge
2	
3	Your task is to build a cross-modal medical image retrieval system. For each query brain MRI volume, rank all candidate target MRI volumes from the matching gallery so that the true same-subject target appears as high as possible.
4	
5	## Kaggle Challenge
6	
7	https://www.kaggle.com/t/b33ec3e76c3d4e16a6b56852470b3ebf
8	
9	
10	## Modalities
11	
12	- Query: T1 post-contrast MRI
13	- Target: T2 MRI
14	
15	All images are 3D NIfTI `.nii.gz` volumes converted to RAS orientation.
16	
17	## Data Layout
18	
19	The data is split into three independent datasets:
20	
21	```text
22	dataset1/
23	  train_pairs.csv
24	  val_queries.csv
25	  val_gallery.csv
26	  test_queries.csv
27	  test_gallery.csv
28	  images/
29	    train/
30	    val/
31	    test/
32	
33	dataset2/
34	  val_queries.csv
35	  val_gallery.csv
36	  test_queries.csv
37	  test_gallery.csv
38	  images/
39	    val/
40	    test/
41	
42	dataset3/
43	  val_queries.csv
44	  val_gallery.csv
45	  test_queries.csv
46	  test_gallery.csv
47	  images/
48	    val/
49	    test/
50	
51	sample_submission.csv
52	```
53	
54	`dataset1` includes labelled training pairs. `dataset2` and `dataset3` have no labelled training pairs and are intended to evaluate generalization.
55	
56	## Dataset Descriptions
57	
58	### Dataset 1
59	
60	`dataset1` contains preoperative MRI pairs only. It provides the labelled training set for the challenge: each row in `dataset1/train_pairs.csv` gives a matching T1 post-contrast query image and T2 target image from the same subject.
61	
62	All `dataset1` pairs, including training, validation, and test pairs, are registered to a common image grid. You may use that fact when training on the labelled pairs and when developing methods on this dataset.
63	
64	`dataset1` validation and test data are provided as query/gallery retrieval pools. The correct matches are hidden and are used for leaderboard scoring.
65	
66	### Dataset 2
67	
68	`dataset2` contains preoperative MRI pairs from the same source setting as `dataset1`, but the validation and test images have random rigid rotation/translation and non-linear deformations applied. Query and target images in a correct pair are deformed independently, so they no longer share one common geometry.
69	
70	No labelled training pairs are provided for `dataset2`. It is intended to test whether a method can generalize from the registered development data to a setting with synthetic geometric variation.
71	
72	The example below shows one correct query-target pair from `dataset2` using one representative slice from each volume:
73	
74	![Dataset 2 correct pair example](assets/dataset2_example_pair.png)
75	
76	### Dataset 3
77	
78	`dataset3` contains preoperative-to-intraoperative MRI pairs. No labelled training pairs are provided for `dataset3`; it is intended to evaluate generalization to a more structurally different setting.
79	
80	In `dataset3`, each intraoperative target image has been resampled into the same geometric space as its matching preoperative query image using the source image physical coordinates. This does not mean the images are registered in the strict sense. The candidates are intraoperative images, so the anatomy can be structurally different from the preoperative query: tissue may have shifted, parts of the brain may be missing, and local structures can look different because of the intervention. The goal is still to retrieve the matching subject, but exact local alignment is not guaranteed.
81	
82	The example below shows one correct query-target pair from `dataset3` using one representative slice from each volume:
83	
84	![Dataset 3 correct pair example](assets/dataset3_example_pair.png)
85	
86	### Preprocessing Notes
87	
88	All images have been converted to NIfTI, RAS orientation, and 1.0 x 1.0 x 1.0 mm voxel spacing. No intensity normalization, histogram matching, skull stripping, deformable registration, or cropping has been applied as part of this release.
89	
90	Your code should not assume one fixed image shape for the whole challenge. Matching query and target volumes may also differ in shape, especially in `dataset2` and `dataset3`.
91	
92	## Files
93	
94	### Training
95	
96	`dataset1/train_pairs.csv` contains labelled query-target pairs:
97	
98	```text
99	pair_id,query_id,target_id,query_image,target_image,query_modality,target_modality,dataset
100	```
101	
102	### Query Manifests
103	
104	Validation and test query files contain:
105	
106	```text
107	query_id,query_image,query_modality,dataset
108	```
109	
110	### Gallery Manifests
111	
112	Validation and test gallery files contain:
113	
114	```text
115	target_id,target_image,target_modality,dataset
116	```
117	
118	## Retrieval Pools
119	
120	The three datasets are independent retrieval pools. Always rank a query only against the gallery from the same dataset and same split:
121	
122	- `dataset1/val_queries.csv` uses `dataset1/val_gallery.csv`
123	- `dataset1/test_queries.csv` uses `dataset1/test_gallery.csv`
124	- `dataset2/val_queries.csv` uses `dataset2/val_gallery.csv`
125	- `dataset2/test_queries.csv` uses `dataset2/test_gallery.csv`
126	- `dataset3/val_queries.csv` uses `dataset3/val_gallery.csv`
127	- `dataset3/test_queries.csv` uses `dataset3/test_gallery.csv`
128	
129	Do not rank queries from one dataset against another dataset's gallery, and do not mix validation and test galleries.
130	
131	## Counts
132	
133	```text
134	dataset1:
135	  train pairs: 350
136	  validation queries/gallery: 40 / 40
137	  test queries/gallery: 100 / 100
138	
139	dataset2:
140	  validation queries/gallery: 40 / 40
141	  test queries/gallery: 100 / 100
142	
143	dataset3:
144	  validation queries/gallery: 20 / 20
145	  test queries/gallery: 77 / 77
146	```
147	
148	## Evaluation
149	
150	The score is mean reciprocal rank (MRR), computed separately for `dataset1`, `dataset2`, and `dataset3`, then averaged:
151	
152	```text
153	score = (dataset1_MRR + dataset2_MRR + dataset3_MRR) / 3
154	```
155	
156	For each query, reciprocal rank is `1 / rank` of the true matching target in the submitted ranking. If the true target is absent, or if the query row is omitted from the submission, that query receives reciprocal rank `0`.
157	
158	Kaggle uses the hidden solution file to decide which rows are public and private:
159	
160	- validation query rows are scored on the public leaderboard during the competition
161	- test query rows are scored on the private leaderboard for final ranking
162	
163	Participants do not include a split column. Kaggle aligns rows by `query_id`, then scores the public and private subsets internally.
164	
165	## Submission Format
166	
167	**Attention: Kaggle comes with a limitation of 100 submissions per team per day!**
168	
169	Kaggle expects one submission file per attempt. Submit one combined CSV with the same columns as the root-level `sample_submission.csv`; do not submit separate files for individual datasets.
170	
171	```text
172	query_id,target_id_ranking
173	q_...,g_... g_... g_... ...
174	```
175	
176	All `query_id` and `target_id` values are globally unique across all three datasets, so the combined file can include rows from `dataset1`, `dataset2`, and `dataset3` without an extra dataset column.
177

file.content:
# Brain MRI Cross-Modal Retrieval Challenge

Your task is to build a cross-modal medical image retrieval system. For each query brain MRI volume, rank all candidate target MRI volumes from the matching gallery so that the true same-subject target appears as high as possible.

## Kaggle Challenge

https://www.kaggle.com/t/b33ec3e76c3d4e16a6b56852470b3ebf


## Modalities

- Query: T1 post-contrast MRI
- Target: T2 MRI

All images are 3D NIfTI `.nii.gz` volumes converted to RAS orientation.

## Data Layout

The data is split into three independent datasets:

```text
dataset1/
  train_pairs.csv
  val_queries.csv
  val_gallery.csv
  test_queries.csv
  test_gallery.csv
  images/
    train/
    val/
    test/

dataset2/
  val_queries.csv
  val_gallery.csv
  test_queries.csv
  test_gallery.csv
  images/
    val/
    test/

dataset3/
  val_queries.csv
  val_gallery.csv
  test_queries.csv
  test_gallery.csv
  images/
    val/
    test/

sample_submission.csv
```

`dataset1` includes labelled training pairs. `dataset2` and `dataset3` have no labelled training pairs and are intended to evaluate generalization.

## Dataset Descriptions

### Dataset 1

`dataset1` contains preoperative MRI pairs only. It provides the labelled training set for the challenge: each row in `dataset1/train_pairs.csv` gives a matching T1 post-contrast query image and T2 target image from the same subject.

All `dataset1` pairs, including training, validation, and test pairs, are registered to a common image grid. You may use that fact when training on the labelled pairs and when developing methods on this dataset.

`dataset1` validation and test data are provided as query/gallery retrieval pools. The correct matches are hidden and are used for leaderboard scoring.

### Dataset 2

`dataset2` contains preoperative MRI pairs from the same source setting as `dataset1`, but the validation and test images have random rigid rotation/translation and non-linear deformations applied. Query and target images in a correct pair are deformed independently, so they no longer share one common geometry.

No labelled training pairs are provided for `dataset2`. It is intended to test whether a method can generalize from the registered development data to a setting with synthetic geometric variation.

The example below shows one correct query-target pair from `dataset2` using one representative slice from each volume:

![Dataset 2 correct pair example](assets/dataset2_example_pair.png)

### Dataset 3

`dataset3` contains preoperative-to-intraoperative MRI pairs. No labelled training pairs are provided for `dataset3`; it is intended to evaluate generalization to a more structurally different setting.

In `dataset3`, each intraoperative target image has been resampled into the same geometric space as its matching preoperative query image using the source image physical coordinates. This does not mean the images are registered in the strict sense. The candidates are intraoperative images, so the anatomy can be structurally different from the preoperative query: tissue may have shifted, parts of the brain may be missing, and local structures can look different because of the intervention. The goal is still to retrieve the matching subject, but exact local alignment is not guaranteed.

The example below shows one correct query-target pair from `dataset3` using one representative slice from each volume:

![Dataset 3 correct pair example](assets/dataset3_example_pair.png)

### Preprocessing Notes

All images have been converted to NIfTI, RAS orientation, and 1.0 x 1.0 x 1.0 mm voxel spacing. No intensity normalization, histogram matching, skull stripping, deformable registration, or cropping has been applied as part of this release.

Your code should not assume one fixed image shape for the whole challenge. Matching query and target volumes may also differ in shape, especially in `dataset2` and `dataset3`.

## Files

### Training

`dataset1/train_pairs.csv` contains labelled query-target pairs:

```text
pair_id,query_id,target_id,query_image,target_image,query_modality,target_modality,dataset
```

### Query Manifests

Validation and test query files contain:

```text
query_id,query_image,query_modality,dataset
```

### Gallery Manifests

Validation and test gallery files contain:

```text
target_id,target_image,target_modality,dataset
```

## Retrieval Pools

The three datasets are independent retrieval pools. Always rank a query only against the gallery from the same dataset and same split:

- `dataset1/val_queries.csv` uses `dataset1/val_gallery.csv`
- `dataset1/test_queries.csv` uses `dataset1/test_gallery.csv`
- `dataset2/val_queries.csv` uses `dataset2/val_gallery.csv`
- `dataset2/test_queries.csv` uses `dataset2/test_gallery.csv`
- `dataset3/val_queries.csv` uses `dataset3/val_gallery.csv`
- `dataset3/test_queries.csv` uses `dataset3/test_gallery.csv`

Do not rank queries from one dataset against another dataset's gallery, and do not mix validation and test galleries.

## Counts

```text
dataset1:
  train pairs: 350
  validation queries/gallery: 40 / 40
  test queries/gallery: 100 / 100

dataset2:
  validation queries/gallery: 40 / 40
  test queries/gallery: 100 / 100

dataset3:
  validation queries/gallery: 20 / 20
  test queries/gallery: 77 / 77
```

## Evaluation

The score is mean reciprocal rank (MRR), computed separately for `dataset1`, `dataset2`, and `dataset3`, then averaged:

```text
score = (dataset1_MRR + dataset2_MRR + dataset3_MRR) / 3
```

For each query, reciprocal rank is `1 / rank` of the true matching target in the submitted ranking. If the true target is absent, or if the query row is omitted from the submission, that query receives reciprocal rank `0`.

Kaggle uses the hidden solution file to decide which rows are public and private:

- validation query rows are scored on the public leaderboard during the competition
- test query rows are scored on the private leaderboard for final ranking

Participants do not include a split column. Kaggle aligns rows by `query_id`, then scores the public and private subsets internally.

## Submission Format

**Attention: Kaggle comes with a limitation of 100 submissions per team per day!**

Kaggle expects one submission file per attempt. Submit one combined CSV with the same columns as the root-level `sample_submission.csv`; do not submit separate files for individual datasets.

```text
query_id,target_id_ranking
q_...,g_... g_... g_... ...
```

All `query_id` and `target_id` values are globally unique across all three datasets, so the combined file can include rows from `dataset1`, `dataset2`, and `dataset3` without an extra dataset column.

Each submitted row must contain a full ranking of every target ID from that query's corresponding same-dataset, same-split gallery. Rankings are space-separated and ordered from most likely match to least likely match.

For example, a query from `dataset2/test_queries.csv` must rank all target IDs from `dataset2/test_gallery.csv`, and no target IDs from any validation gallery or from another dataset:

```text
query_id,target_id_ranking
q_example_dataset2_test,g_first_choice g_second_choice g_third_choice ...
```

Expected ranking lengths:

```text
dataset1 validation rows: 40 target IDs
dataset1 test rows: 100 target IDs
dataset2 validation rows: 40 target IDs
dataset2 test rows: 100 target IDs
dataset3 validation rows: 20 target IDs
dataset3 test rows: 77 target IDs
```

The complete submission template contains one row for every validation and test query from all three datasets, for `377` rows total. Partial submissions are allowed:

- For validation-only experiments, submit rows from `val_queries.csv` files.
- For full challenge submissions, submit both validation and test query rows in one file.
- To focus on one dataset first, submit only that dataset's rows and omit the other datasets. The omitted datasets receive zero credit. Multiplying the displayed score by `3` gives the MRR for the submitted dataset.

## Baseline Code

We provide a small MONAI + PyTorch baseline to help you get started with the challenge data format, preprocessing, training loop, and submission generation.

The baseline is intentionally simple. It is not meant to be a strong solution. Its purpose is to demonstrate:

* how to load 3D NIfTI volumes with MONAI
* how to apply basic medical image preprocessing and create a quick-to-load cache
* how to write a valid Kaggle submission file
* the task complexity of the different datasets

Install and run it with uv:

```sh
DATA_ROOT=/path/to/kaggle_dataset
uv run slice_clip_baseline.py \
  --data-root "$DATA_ROOT" \
  --train-pair-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/train_pairs.csv" \
  --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/val_queries.csv" \
  --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/val_gallery.csv" \
  --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/test_queries.csv" \
  --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/test_gallery.csv" \
  --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/val_queries.csv" \
  --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/val_gallery.csv" \
  --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/test_queries.csv" \
  --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/test_gallery.csv" \
  --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/val_queries.csv" \
  --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/val_gallery.csv" \
  --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/test_queries.csv" \
  --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/test_gallery.csv" \
  --out slice_clip_submission.csv
```

This writes a combined submission file:

`slice_clip_submission.csv`

The file can be submitted directly to Kaggle.

You can also submit only one dataset to get intuition for the difficulty of each retrieval pool.
For example, to train on dataset1 and submit only dataset1 validation and test rows:

```sh
DATA_ROOT=/path/to/kaggle_dataset
uv run slice_cnn_baseline.py \
  --data-root "$DATA_ROOT" \
  --train-pair-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/train_pairs.csv" \
  --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/val_queries.csv" \
  --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/val_gallery.csv" \
  --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/test_queries.csv" \
  --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/test_gallery.csv" \
  --out dataset1_submission.csv
```

content.content:
{"additions":18,"baseRefName":"main","body":"## What this does

Two small changes so the baseline (`slice_clip_baseline.py`) can actually run on our GPU server with the real competition data.

### 1. Use the GPU when there is one
Before, the script only knew about Apple's `mps` chip or the `cpu`. Our training box is an AMD Instinct MI300X, so we now pick the GPU automatically (`cuda`, which also covers AMD/ROCm), and still fall back to `mps` or `cpu` elsewhere. No other behaviour changes.

### 2. Find the image files whether they end in `.nii` or `.nii.gz`
The competition's list of files (the CSV manifests) names every volume with a `.nii.gz` ending, but the files we actually downloaded are plain, uncompressed `.nii`. That mismatch made the script unable to find any image. Now, if the exact path isn't on disk, it simply tries the other ending. Nothing changes when the names already match.

## Why
Without these, the baseline can't train or even open the data on the server.

## Result
With these changes the baseline trained end-to-end on the MI300X and produced a valid submission (377 ranked queries, no empty rows). It scored **0.37659** on the public leaderboard — our first score on the board. Full dataset findings are in the linked issue.

## Notes
- The diff is intentionally tiny (18 lines). I did **not** touch the pre-existing lint/style of the file.
- Added `samples/` to `.gitignore` so the local sample volumes I downloaded for visualization don't get committed.

Summary of what the data looks like, the statistics I gathered, and the result of the first baseline run on the GPU. Tagging @Gklajer and @gablandman for review.

## The task in one line
For each **query** brain MRI (T1 post-contrast), rank all **gallery** MRIs (T2) so the same patient's scan appears as high as possible. Score = **Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR)**, averaged over three datasets.

## The data at a glance

There are **3 independent datasets**, each scored separately. You always rank a query only against the gallery of the **same dataset and same split** (never mix them).

| Dataset | What's special | train pairs | val (q / gallery) | test (q / gallery) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **dataset1** | Clean, all volumes registered to one common grid | **350** | 40 / 40 | 100 / 100 |
| **dataset2** | Same source as ds1, but val/test images get **random rotations/shifts + non-linear warps** (query and target warped *independently*) | none | 40 / 40 | 100 / 100 |
| **dataset3** | **Pre-op → intra-op** pairs: anatomy can genuinely differ (tissue shifts, missing structures) | none | 20 / 20 | 77 / 77 |

- Only **dataset1 has labelled training pairs (350)**. dataset2 and dataset3 give us *no* labels — they test whether a method **generalizes**.
- Modalities: query = **T1 post-contrast**, target = **T2** for every labelled pair.
- Total: **1,454 NIfTI volumes** (~17 MB each, ~10.4 GB zipped).

## Statistics I measured

**Image geometry** (all volumes are 1.0×1.0×1.0 mm):
- **dataset1 & dataset2**: every volume is exactly **240 × 240 × 155**.
- **dataset3**: shapes **vary** — e.g. 211×250×192, 219×250×192, 256×256×192, 211×250×176. So any model must **not assume a fixed shape** (the README warns about this; confirmed here).

**Intensity** (important): **no normalization has been applied**. Minimum is always 0, but the maximum swings wildly across volumes (I saw maxima from ~210 to ~13,775). Per-volume intensity scaling is therefore essential — the baseline already does min-max scaling per image.

**How hard is random guessing?** Expected MRR if we ranked randomly:

| Split | Gallery size | Random-guess MRR |
|---|---|---|
| ds1/ds2 val | 40 | ~0.107 |
| ds1/ds2 test | 100 | ~0.052 |
| ds3 val | 20 | ~0.180 |
| ds3 test | 77 | ~0.064 |

So anything meaningfully above ~0.05–0.10 per split is doing real work.

## Example pair (visualization)

I downloaded one labelled dataset1 pair and rendered 3 axial slices of each. You can clearly see the **T1c (query)** vs **T2 (target)** contrast difference for the same subject, including a visible lesion. (Rendered locally to `samples/sample_pair_viz.png` via `samples/viz_sample.py` — not committed since it's data scratch. Happy to attach the PNG here if useful.)

## Gotcha worth knowing
The CSV manifests list every file as **`.nii.gz`**, but the files actually shipped are uncompressed **`.nii`**. Code that trusts the manifest path verbatim finds **zero** images. Fixed in #1 by falling back to whichever extension exists.

## First baseline result
Ran the provided `slice_clip_baseline.py` (MONAI 3-slice CNN, dual encoder, CLIP-style in-batch contrastive loss) on our **AMD MI300X** (ROCm PyTorch 2.10).
- Trained end-to-end on the 350 dataset1 pairs (loss 4.77 → 0.66 over 500 epochs).
- Produced a valid combined submission: **377 ranked queries, 0 empty rows**.
- **Public leaderboard score: 0.37659** — our first score on the board (team BRAINROT-LABS). For reference, current top is ~0.89.

## Ideas for improving the score (for discussion)
1. **Use 3D structure**, not just 3 middle slices — the encoders throw away most of the volume.
2. **Pretrained medical encoders** (the MI300X has 192 GB, so big models fit easily).
3. **Robustness for dataset2/3** — augment training with the same rigid+elastic deformations dataset2 applies, since we only train on clean dataset1.
4. **A single shared encoder** or stronger cross-modal alignment instead of two separate tiny CNNs.
5. **Validate locally on the val splits** before each submission (we get public-LB feedback only on val rows, limited to 100 subs/day).

---
Environment + reproduction details are in PR #1. @Gklajer @gablandman — please review the findings and the direction above.

content.text:
Yes — almost certainly a bijection, and **no**, the predictions are not independent.

**Evidence of bijection** (from your PR/README):
- Gallery size = query size in every split (ds1 val 40/40, test 100/100; ds2 40/40, 100/100; ds3 20/20, 77/77).
- Wording says *the* true same-subject target (singular). One query → one target, and one target serves one query.
- You can confirm in 10s on `dataset1/train_pairs.csv`: `query_id` and `target_id` should both be unique → bijective on the labelled set.

**Are queries independent at scoring time?**
MRR is `mean_q (1/rank_q)` over a *per-query full permutation of the gallery*. So yes, scoring is per-query: the gallery items must all appear in each query's ranking — you literally cannot "exclude" one.

**But predictions are not independent — you should exploit the bijection at ranking time.** If you're highly confident `q_A → t_X`, then under a true 1-1 mapping `t_X` cannot be the match for any other query, so demote `t_X` to the bottom of every other query's ranking. This is just a linear assignment problem on the similarity matrix `S[i,j]`:

1. Compute the full `N × N` similarity.
2. Either:
   - **Hungarian / Jonker-Volgenant** on `-S` → forces one-to-one for the top of each query's ranking; rank the rest by raw similarity.
   - **Sinkhorn normalization** (a few iterations of row/col rescaling) → soft doubly-stochastic scores that already encode "competition" between queries for the same target. Often easier to plug in.
3. For each query: put the assigned target first, then the remaining gallery sorted by the (post-Sinkhorn) score with the assigned column masked out.

**Caveat where independence still matters:**
- ds2 has independent warps on query and target → similarity may be noisier; bad early assignments propagate. Greedy "confident-first" matching with backtracking, or iterative Sinkhorn, is safer than pure Hungarian.
- ds3 has 20/77 sized galleries — small, so joint matching cheap and very high-leverage.

Quick win: take the current 0.37659 submission, build `S`, run Hungarian per dataset/split, rerank — that alone usually moves MRR up several points without retraining.

Bijection confirmed on training data, and every val/test split has the same `|Q| = |G|` clean structure. Summary:

| Split | \|Q\| | \|G\| | duplicates | query modality | target modality |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ds1 train_pairs | 350 | 350 | none | T1c | T2 |
| ds1 val / test | 40 / 100 | 40 / 100 | none | T1c | T2 |
| ds2 val / test | 40 / 100 | 40 / 100 | none | T1c | T2 |
| ds3 val / test | 20 / 77 | 20 / 77 | none | T1c | T2 |

- Across the entire challenge: **727 unique query_ids, 727 unique target_ids, zero overlap** between the two id spaces. So a target_id is never reused as a query_id, and within each split every id appears exactly once.
- Every query is `T1 post-contrast`, every target is `T2`. No modality leakage.
- On the labelled `dataset1/train_pairs.csv`: each of the 350 queries matches a unique target — strict bijection on the labelled set.

Combined with the README's "the true same-subject target" wording (singular) and the fact that `|Q| = |G|` in every hidden split, this is as strong as it gets without seeing the hidden labels: **assume a one-to-one match in every split**. Hungarian / Sinkhorn assignment is well-justified.

Want me to go ahead with both rerankers on the GPU now? I'd:
1. Look at `slice_clip_baseline.py` to see whether it can dump per-pair similarities (probably needs a small extension since it directly writes ranks).
2. Re-run inference with the trained encoders to get `S[i,j]` matrices per (dataset, split).
3. Generate two submissions — `submission_hungarian.csv` and `submission_sinkhorn.csv` — plus the original for comparison.
4. Quick sanity check by running the same reranker on the train pairs (where we have ground truth) to confirm it doesn't degrade.

content.text:
You're right, and that risk is real at our current MRR level.

**Quantifying the risk.** At MRR 0.376, the model's top-1 is probably ~25–35% accurate. Hungarian forces a permutation, so every wrong "confident" top-1 doesn't just hurt that query — it *removes* the true target from someone else's ranking and pushes it to the bottom. The errors are correlated and cascade. Independent ranking is "embarrassingly parallel" and at least lets each query make its own mistake.

**Hungarian helps only when top-1 is already strong** (rule of thumb: ≥70% top-1). At 30% top-1 it can absolutely make MRR worse.

**Sinkhorn is the safer middle ground.** It doesn't *commit* — it just rescales the score matrix so a target that looks similar to many queries gets damped. It softly enforces "queries compete for the same target" but never sets rank-1 in stone. With a high temperature / few iterations it degrades gracefully toward independent.

**A compromise that addresses your concern directly: confidence-gated assignment.**

1. Look at the *margin* per query: `s_top1 - s_top2`.
2. Only commit assignments where the margin is large (e.g., top 10–20% most confident queries).
3. For those, do greedy assignment (or Hungarian on the confident subset).
4. For the rest, keep independent ranking.

That captures the upside of the bijection where the model is *actually* confident, and refuses to risk the cascade where it isn't.

**How to decide for real.** We have labelled data — `dataset1/train_pairs.csv` (350 pairs). Concrete plan:
- Hold out, say, 20% of train pairs the model has already seen, but at least we can sanity-check direction.
- Better: dump the val similarity matrix (40×40 on ds1, ds2 each, 20×20 on ds3), build all four submissions (independent / Sinkhorn / Hungarian / confidence-gated), and submit. Each public-LB score *is* the val MRR. We have 100 submissions/day.

Given the noise, my recommendation is: don't pick by argument — run all four, look at the LB, and let the data answer. Want me to set that up?

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	# /// script
3	# requires-python = ">=3.12"
4	# dependencies = [
5	#   "monai>=1.5.0",
6	#   "torch>=2.7.0",
7	#   "numpy>=2.0",
8	#   "nibabel>=5.3",
9	#   "tqdm>=4.67",
10	# ]
11	# ///
12	
13	"""
14	Tiny 2D slice CLIP baseline for the Brain MRI Cross-Modal Retrieval Challenge.
15	
16	The script demonstrates:
17	- MONAI loading, channel handling, 1 mm spacing, intensity scaling, slice
18	  extraction, resizing, typing, and PersistentDataset caching.
19	- A tiny random image-noise augmentation during training.
20	- Two small 2D CNN encoders trained with a CLIP-style in-batch contrastive loss.
21	- Submission generation by ranking gallery targets by embedding similarity.
22	
23	Run from this folder with uv. This trains only on the labelled dataset1
24	training pairs, then writes one combined submission containing validation and
25	test queries for dataset1, dataset2, and dataset3:
26	
27	    DATA_ROOT=/path/to/kaggle_dataset
28	
29	    uv run slice_clip_baseline.py \
30	      --data-root "$DATA_ROOT" \
31	      --train-pair-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/train_pairs.csv" \
32	      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/val_queries.csv" \
33	      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/val_gallery.csv" \
34	      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/test_queries.csv" \
35	      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/test_gallery.csv" \
36	      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/val_queries.csv" \
37	      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/val_gallery.csv" \
38	      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/test_queries.csv" \
39	      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/test_gallery.csv" \
40	      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/val_queries.csv" \
41	      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/val_gallery.csv" \
42	      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/test_queries.csv" \
43	      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/test_gallery.csv" \
44	      --out slice_clip_submission.csv
45	"""
46	
47	import argparse
48	import csv
49	import json
50	import random
51	from pathlib import Path
52	
53	import numpy as np
54	import torch
55	import torch.nn.functional as F
56	from monai.data import PersistentDataset
57	from monai.transforms import (
58	    Compose,
59	    EnsureChannelFirstd,
60	    EnsureTyped,
61	    LoadImaged,
62	    MapTransform,
63	    Orientationd,
64	    RandGaussianNoised,
65	    ScaleIntensityd,
66	    Spacingd,
67	)
68	from torch import nn
69	from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Dataset
70	from tqdm import tqdm
71	
72	
73	CONFIG = {
74	    "seed": 20260626,
75	    "cache_dir": ".monai_persistent",
76	    "spacing_mm": (1.0, 1.0, 1.0),
77	    "slice_positions": (0.35, 0.50, 0.65),
78	    "image_size": 96,
79	    "noise_probability": 0.3,
80	    "noise_std": 0.05,
81	    "epochs": 500,
82	    "batch_size": 128,
83	    "learning_rate": 1e-3,
84	    "embedding_dim": 128,
85	    "encoder_hidden_dim": 512,
86	    "similarity_scale": 5.0,
87	    "max_grad_norm": 1.0,
88	    "num_workers": 0,
89	    "device": (
90	        "cuda"
91	        if torch.cuda.is_available()
92	        else "mps"
93	        if torch.backends.mps.is_available()
94	        else "cpu"
95	    ),
96	}
97	
98	
99	class SliceStackd(MapTransform):
100	    """MONAI map transform for three representative 2D slices."""
101	
102	    def __init__(self, keys: str | list[str], positions: tuple[float, ...], image_size: int) -> None:
103	        """Remember where to sample slices and how large to resize them."""
104	        super().__init__(keys)
105	        self.positions = positions
106	        self.image_size = image_size
107	
108	    def __call__(self, data: dict) -> dict:
109	        """Replace each 3D volume by a resized 3-channel slice stack."""
110	        d = dict(data)
111	        for key in self.key_iterator(d):
112	            volume = torch.as_tensor(d[key]).float()
113	            if volume.ndim != 4:
114	                raise ValueError(f"Expected channel-first 3D volume, got shape {tuple(volume.shape)}")
115	            volume = volume[0]
116	            finite = torch.where(torch.isfinite(volume), volume, torch.zeros_like(volume))
117	            nonzero_counts = torch.count_nonzero(finite, dim=(0, 1))
118	            occupied = torch.nonzero(nonzero_counts, as_tuple=False).flatten()
119	            if len(occupied) == 0:
120	                z_values = [volume.shape[-1] // 2] * len(self.positions)
121	            else:
122	                z_min = int(occupied[0])
123	                z_max = int(occupied[-1])
124	                z_values = [round(z_min + position * (z_max - z_min)) for position in self.positions]
125	
126	            slices = torch.stack([volume[:, :, int(np.clip(z, 0, volume.shape[-1] - 1))] for z in z_values])
127	            slices = torch.nan_to_num(slices, nan=0.0, posinf=0.0, neginf=0.0)
128	            slices = F.interpolate(
129	                slices.unsqueeze(0),
130	                size=(self.image_size, self.image_size),
131	                mode="bilinear",
132	                align_corners=False,
133	            ).squeeze(0)
134	            d[key] = slices
135	        return d
136	
137	
138	class TinySliceEncoder(nn.Module):
139	    """A deliberately small 2D image encoder."""
140	
141	    def __init__(self, embedding_dim: int) -> None:
142	        """Build a small CNN that keeps enough layout to memorize tiny sets."""
143	        super().__init__()
144	        self.features = nn.Sequential(
145	            nn.Conv2d(3, 16, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
146	            nn.ReLU(),
147	            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
148	            nn.Conv2d(16, 32, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
149	            nn.ReLU(),
150	            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
151	            nn.Conv2d(32, 64, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
152	            nn.ReLU(),
153	            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
154	        )
155	        pooled_size = int(CONFIG["image_size"]) // 8
156	        self.projection = nn.Sequential(
157	            nn.Flatten(),
158	            nn.Linear(64 * pooled_size * pooled_size, int(CONFIG["encoder_hidden_dim"])),
159	            nn.ReLU(),
160	            nn.Linear(int(CONFIG["encoder_hidden_dim"]), embedding_dim),
161	        )
162	
163	    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
164	        """Map a 3-slice image stack to an embedding."""
165	        return self.projection(self.features(x))
166	
167	
168	class SliceCLIP(nn.Module):
169	    """Encode query and target images into one shared embedding space."""
170	
171	    def __init__(self, embedding_dim: int) -> None:
172	        """Create two tiny encoders and a fixed similarity scale."""
173	        super().__init__()
174	        self.query_encoder = TinySliceEncoder(embedding_dim)
175	        self.target_encoder = TinySliceEncoder(embedding_dim)
176	        self.similarity_scale = float(CONFIG["similarity_scale"])
177	
178	    def forward(self, query: torch.Tensor, target: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
179	        """Return all query-target similarities in the batch."""
180	        query_embedding = self.encode_query(query)
181	        target_embedding = self.encode_target(target)
182	        return self.similarity_scale * query_embedding @ target_embedding.T
183	
184	    def encode_query(self, query: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
185	        """Encode query images and normalize the embeddings."""
186	        return F.normalize(self.query_encoder(query), dim=1)
187	
188	    def encode_target(self, target: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
189	        """Encode target images and normalize the embeddings."""
190	        return F.normalize(self.target_encoder(target), dim=1)
191	
192	
193	class PairImageDataset(Dataset):
194	    """Training pairs backed by MONAI-cached slice stacks."""
195	
196	    def __init__(self, pairs: list[dict[str, str | Path]], image_dataset: PersistentDataset) -> None:
197	        """Keep only positive pairs; each batch supplies in-batch negatives."""
198	        self.image_dataset = image_dataset
199	        self.id_to_index = {row["id"]: index for index, row in enumerate(image_dataset.data)}
200	        self.examples = [(str(pair["query_id"]), str(pair["target_id"])) for pair in pairs]
201	
202	    def __len__(self) -> int:
203	        """Return the number of positive pair examples."""
204	        return len(self.examples)
205	
206	    def __getitem__(self, index: int) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
207	        """Fetch a query-target image pair; training noise is sampled here."""
208	        query_id, target_id = self.examples[index]
209	        query_image = self._image(query_id)
210	        target_image = self._image(target_id)
211	        return query_image, target_image
212	
213	    def _image(self, image_id: str) -> torch.Tensor:
214	        """Load one cached slice stack by image ID."""
215	        item = self.image_dataset[self.id_to_index[image_id]]
216	        return torch.as_tensor(item["image"]).float()
217	
218	
219	def read_csv(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
220	    """Read a CSV into plain dictionaries."""
221	    with path.open(newline="") as f:
222	        return list(csv.DictReader(f))
223	
224	
225	def resolve_image_path(data_root: Path, image_path: str) -> Path:
226	    """Resolve manifest image paths relative to the dataset root."""
227	    path = Path(image_path)
228	    resolved = path if path.is_absolute() else data_root / path
229	    if resolved.exists():
230	        return resolved
231	    # The manifests list .nii.gz, but the released volumes are uncompressed .nii
232	    # (or vice versa); fall back to whichever extension exists on disk.
233	    text = str(resolved)
234	    alternate = Path(text[:-3]) if text.endswith(".nii.gz") else Path(text + ".gz")
235	    return alternate if alternate.exists() else resolved
236	
237	
238	def load_manifest_images(data_root: Path, csv_paths: list[Path], id_column: str, image_column: str) -> dict[str, Path]:
239	    """Load image IDs and paths from one or more manifest CSVs."""
240	    images: dict[str, Path] = {}
241	    for csv_path in csv_paths:
242	        for row in read_csv(csv_path):
243	            images[row[id_column]] = resolve_image_path(data_root, row[image_column])
244	    return images
245	
246	
247	def load_training_pairs(
248	    data_root: Path,
249	    train_pair_csvs: list[Path],
250	    train_query_csvs: list[Path],
251	    train_gallery_csvs: list[Path],
252	    train_label_csvs: list[Path],
253	) -> list[dict[str, str | Path]]:
254	    """Collect positive training pairs from pair CSVs or labelled pools."""
255	    pairs: list[dict[str, str | Path]] = []
256	    for csv_path in train_pair_csvs:
257	        for row in read_csv(csv_path):
258	            pairs.append(
259	                {
260	                    "query_id": row["query_id"],
261	                    "target_id": row["target_id"],
262	                    "query_path": resolve_image_path(data_root, row["query_image"]),
263	                    "target_path": resolve_image_path(data_root, row["target_image"]),
264	                }
265	            )
266	
267	    query_images = load_manifest_images(data_root, train_query_csvs, "query_id", "query_image")
268	    target_images = load_manifest_images(data_root, train_gallery_csvs, "target_id", "target_image")
269	    for csv_path in train_label_csvs:
270	        for row in read_csv(csv_path):
271	            query_id = row["query_id"]
272	            target_id = row["target_id"]
273	            if query_id not in query_images or target_id not in target_images:
274	                continue
275	            pairs.append(
276	                {
277	                    "query_id": query_id,
278	                    "target_id": target_id,
279	                    "query_path": query_images[query_id],
280	                    "target_path": target_images[target_id],
281	                }
282	            )
283	
284	    if not pairs:
285	        raise ValueError("No training pairs found. Pass --train-pair-csv or query/gallery/label CSVs.")
286	    return pairs
287	
288	
289	def collect_prediction_sets(
290	    data_root: Path,
291	    query_csvs: list[Path],
292	    gallery_csvs: list[Path],
293	) -> list[dict[str, dict[str, Path]]]:
294	    """Pair up query and gallery manifests for ranking."""
295	    if len(query_csvs) != len(gallery_csvs):
296	        raise ValueError("--query-csv and --gallery-csv must be passed the same number of times")
297	
298	    sets = []
299	    for query_csv, gallery_csv in zip(query_csvs, gallery_csvs):
300	        queries = load_manifest_images(data_root, [query_csv], "query_id", "query_image")
301	        targets = load_manifest_images(data_root, [gallery_csv], "target_id", "target_image")
302	        sets.append({"queries": queries, "targets": targets})
303	    return sets
304	
305	
306	def monai_transform(augment: bool) -> Compose:
307	    """Build the MONAI pipeline; optional noise happens after cached slices."""
308	    deterministic = [
309	        LoadImaged(keys="image", image_only=True),
310	        EnsureChannelFirstd(keys="image"),
311	        Orientationd(keys="image", axcodes="RAS", labels=None),
312	        Spacingd(keys="image", pixdim=CONFIG["spacing_mm"], mode="bilinear"),
313	        ScaleIntensityd(keys="image", minv=0.0, maxv=1.0),
314	        SliceStackd(
315	            keys="image",
316	            positions=tuple(CONFIG["slice_positions"]),
317	            image_size=int(CONFIG["image_size"]),
318	        ),
319	        EnsureTyped(keys="image"),
320	    ] # monai persistent dataset knows which transforms are deterministic and caches the results after the last deterministic transform automatically
321	    random_augmentation = []
322	    if augment:
323	        random_augmentation.append(
324	            RandGaussianNoised(
325	                keys="image",
326	                prob=float(CONFIG["noise_probability"]),
327	                mean=0.0,
328	                std=float(CONFIG["noise_std"]),
329	            )
330	        )
331	    return Compose(deterministic + random_augmentation)
332	
333	
334	def make_image_dataset(images: dict[str, Path], example_root: Path, augment: bool) -> PersistentDataset:
335	    """Create the MONAI dataset; it handles cache reads/writes itself."""
336	    cache_dir = example_root / str(CONFIG["cache_dir"])
337	    cache_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
338	    rows = [{"image": str(path), "id": image_id} for image_id, path in sorted(images.items())]
339	    return PersistentDataset(data=rows, transform=monai_transform(augment=augment), cache_dir=cache_dir)
340	
341	
342	def train_model(train_dataset: PairImageDataset) -> SliceCLIP:
343	    """Train the tiny dual encoder with in-batch negatives."""
344	    torch.manual_seed(int(CONFIG["seed"]))
345	    device = torch.device(str(CONFIG["device"]))
346	    model = SliceCLIP(int(CONFIG["embedding_dim"])).to(device)
347	    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=float(CONFIG["learning_rate"]))
348	    loss_fn = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
349	    loader = DataLoader(
350	        train_dataset,
351	        batch_size=int(CONFIG["batch_size"]),
352	        shuffle=True,
353	        num_workers=int(CONFIG["num_workers"]),
354	    )
355	
356	    model.train()
357	    for epoch in range(1, int(CONFIG["epochs"]) + 1):
358	        total_loss = 0.0
359	        total_seen = 0
360	        for query_batch, target_batch in loader:
361	            query_batch = query_batch.to(device)
362	            target_batch = target_batch.to(device)
363	            labels = torch.arange(len(query_batch), device=device)
364	            optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
365	            logits = model(query_batch, target_batch)
366	            loss = (loss_fn(logits, labels) + loss_fn(logits.T, labels)) / 2
367	            loss.backward()
368	            nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), max_norm=float(CONFIG["max_grad_norm"]))
369	            optimizer.step()
370	            total_loss += float(loss.item()) * len(query_batch)
371	            total_seen += len(query_batch)
372	        print(f"epoch {epoch:03d} loss={total_loss / max(total_seen, 1):.5f}")
373	    return model
374	
375	
376	@torch.no_grad()
377	def embed_images(model: SliceCLIP, image_dataset: PersistentDataset, encoder: str) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
378	    """Run one encoder once for each deterministic query/gallery image."""
379	    device = next(model.parameters()).device
380	    loader = DataLoader(image_dataset, batch_size=int(CONFIG["batch_size"]), shuffle=False)
381	    embeddings: dict[str, np.ndarray] = {}
382	    model.eval()
383	    for batch in tqdm(loader, desc="Embedding images"):
384	        image_batch = torch.as_tensor(batch["image"]).float().to(device)
385	        if encoder == "query":
386	            embedding_batch = model.encode_query(image_batch).detach().cpu().numpy().astype(np.float32)
387	        elif encoder == "target":
388	            embedding_batch = model.encode_target(image_batch).detach().cpu().numpy().astype(np.float32)
389	        else:
390	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown encoder: {encoder}")
391	        for image_id, embedding in zip(batch["id"], embedding_batch):
392	            embeddings[str(image_id)] = embedding
393	    return embeddings
394	
395	
396	@torch.no_grad()
397	def rank_targets(
398	    model: SliceCLIP,
399	    query_embeddings: dict[str, np.ndarray],
400	    target_embeddings: dict[str, np.ndarray],
401	) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
402	    """Score every query against every target and return rankings."""
403	    device = next(model.parameters()).device
404	    model.eval()
405	    rows: list[dict[str, str]] = []
406	    target_ids = sorted(target_embeddings)
407	    target_matrix = torch.from_numpy(np.stack([target_embeddings[target_id] for target_id in target_ids])).to(device)
408	
409	    for query_id in tqdm(sorted(query_embeddings), desc="Ranking queries"):
410	        query_embedding = torch.from_numpy(query_embeddings[query_id]).to(device)
411	        scores = (model.similarity_scale * query_embedding.unsqueeze(0) @ target_matrix.T).squeeze(0)
412	        scores = scores.detach().cpu().numpy()
413	        ranking = [target_ids[index] for index in np.argsort(-scores)]
414	        rows.append({"query_id": query_id, "target_id_ranking": " ".join(ranking)})
415	    return rows
416	
417	
418	def write_submission(path: Path, rows: list[dict[str, str]]) -> None:
419	    """Write Kaggle-style query rankings."""
420	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
421	    with path.open("w", newline="") as f:
422	        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=["query_id", "target_id_ranking"])
423	        writer.writeheader()
424	        writer.writerows(rows)
425	
426	
427	def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
428	    """Parse the few CSV paths needed to train and rank."""
429	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Simple MONAI + middle-slice CNN retrieval baseline.")
430	    parser.add_argument("--data-root", type=Path, required=True)
431	    parser.add_argument("--train-pair-csv", type=Path, action="append", default=[])
432	    parser.add_argument("--train-query-csv", type=Path, action="append", default=[])
433	    parser.add_argument("--train-gallery-csv", type=Path, action="append", default=[])
434	    parser.add_argument("--train-label-csv", type=Path, action="append", default=[])
435	    parser.add_argument("--query-csv", type=Path, action="append", required=True)
436	    parser.add_argument("--gallery-csv", type=Path, action="append", required=True)
437	    parser.add_argument("--out", type=Path, default=Path("slice_cnn_submission.csv"))
438	    return parser.parse_args()
439	
440	
441	def main() -> None:
442	    """Wire the baseline together from CSVs to submission file."""
443	    args = parse_args()
444	    random.seed(int(CONFIG["seed"]))
445	    np.random.seed(int(CONFIG["seed"]))
446	
447	    example_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
448	    data_root = args.data_root.resolve()
449	    train_pairs = load_training_pairs(
450	        data_root,
451	        args.train_pair_csv,
452	        args.train_query_csv,
453	        args.train_gallery_csv,
454	        args.train_label_csv,
455	    )
456	    prediction_sets = collect_prediction_sets(data_root, args.query_csv, args.gallery_csv)
457	
458	    train_images: dict[str, Path] = {}
459	    for pair in train_pairs:
460	        train_images[str(pair["query_id"])] = Path(pair["query_path"])
461	        train_images[str(pair["target_id"])] = Path(pair["target_path"])
462	
463	    inference_images: dict[str, Path] = {}
464	    for prediction_set in prediction_sets:
465	        inference_images.update(prediction_set["queries"])
466	        inference_images.update(prediction_set["targets"])
467	
468	    print(
469	        json.dumps(
470	            {
471	                "config": CONFIG,
472	                "num_train_images": len(train_images),
473	                "num_inference_images": len(inference_images),
474	                "num_train_pairs": len(train_pairs),
475	            },
476	            indent=2,
477	        )
478	    )
479	    train_image_dataset = make_image_dataset(train_images, example_root, augment=True)
480	    inference_image_dataset = make_image_dataset(inference_images, example_root, augment=False)
481	    train_dataset = PairImageDataset(train_pairs, train_image_dataset)
482	    model = train_model(train_dataset)
483	    query_embeddings_all = embed_images(model, inference_image_dataset, encoder="query")
484	    target_embeddings_all = embed_images(model, inference_image_dataset, encoder="target")
485	
486	    submission_rows: list[dict[str, str]] = []
487	    for prediction_set in prediction_sets:
488	        query_embeddings = {image_id: query_embeddings_all[image_id] for image_id in prediction_set["queries"]}
489	        target_embeddings = {image_id: target_embeddings_all[image_id] for image_id in prediction_set["targets"]}
490	        submission_rows.extend(rank_targets(model, query_embeddings, target_embeddings))
491	
492	    write_submission(args.out, submission_rows)
493	    print(f"Wrote {len(submission_rows)} rows to {args.out}")
494	
495

from __future__ import annotations
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.12"
# dependencies = [
#   "monai>=1.5.0",
#   "torch>=2.7.0",
#   "numpy>=2.0",
#   "nibabel>=5.3",
#   "tqdm>=4.67",
# ]
# ///

"""
Tiny 2D slice CLIP baseline for the Brain MRI Cross-Modal Retrieval Challenge.

The script demonstrates:
- MONAI loading, channel handling, 1 mm spacing, intensity scaling, slice
  extraction, resizing, typing, and PersistentDataset caching.
- A tiny random image-noise augmentation during training.
- Two small 2D CNN encoders trained with a CLIP-style in-batch contrastive loss.
- Submission generation by ranking gallery targets by embedding similarity.

Run from this folder with uv. This trains only on the labelled dataset1
training pairs, then writes one combined submission containing validation and
test queries for dataset1, dataset2, and dataset3:

    DATA_ROOT=/path/to/kaggle_dataset

    uv run slice_clip_baseline.py \
      --data-root "$DATA_ROOT" \
      --train-pair-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/train_pairs.csv" \
      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/val_queries.csv" \
      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/val_gallery.csv" \
      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/test_queries.csv" \
      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset1/test_gallery.csv" \
      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/val_queries.csv" \
      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/val_gallery.csv" \
      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/test_queries.csv" \
      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset2/test_gallery.csv" \
      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/val_queries.csv" \
      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/val_gallery.csv" \
      --query-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/test_queries.csv" \
      --gallery-csv "$DATA_ROOT/dataset3/test_gallery.csv" \
      --out slice_clip_submission.csv
"""

import argparse
import csv
import json
import random
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F
from monai.data import PersistentDataset
from monai.transforms import (
    Compose,
    EnsureChannelFirstd,
    EnsureTyped,
    LoadImaged,
    MapTransform,
    Orientationd,
    RandGaussianNoised,
    ScaleIntensityd,
    Spacingd,
)
from torch import nn
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Dataset
from tqdm import tqdm


CONFIG = {
    "seed": 20260626,
    "cache_dir": ".monai_persistent",
    "spacing_mm": (1.0, 1.0, 1.0),
    "slice_positions": (0.35, 0.50, 0.65),
    "image_size": 96,
    "noise_probability": 0.3,
    "noise_std": 0.05,
    "epochs": 500,
    "batch_size": 128,
    "learning_rate": 1e-3,
    "embedding_dim": 128,
    "encoder_hidden_dim": 512,
    "similarity_scale": 5.0,
    "max_grad_norm": 1.0,
    "num_workers": 0,
    "device": (
        "cuda"
        if torch.cuda.is_available()
        else "mps"
        if torch.backends.mps.is_available()
        else "cpu"
    ),
}


class SliceStackd(MapTransform):
    """MONAI map transform for three representative 2D slices."""

    def __init__(self, keys: str | list[str], positions: tuple[float, ...], image_size: int) -> None:
        """Remember where to sample slices and how large to resize them."""
        super().__init__(keys)
        self.positions = positions
        self.image_size = image_size

    def __call__(self, data: dict) -> dict:
        """Replace each 3D volume by a resized 3-channel slice stack."""
        d = dict(data)
        for key in self.key_iterator(d):
            volume = torch.as_tensor(d[key]).float()
            if volume.ndim != 4:
                raise ValueError(f"Expected channel-first 3D volume, got shape {tuple(volume.shape)}")
            volume = volume[0]
            finite = torch.where(torch.isfinite(volume), volume, torch.zeros_like(volume))
            nonzero_counts = torch.count_nonzero(finite, dim=(0, 1))
            occupied = torch.nonzero(nonzero_counts, as_tuple=False).flatten()
            if len(occupied) == 0:
                z_values = [volume.shape[-1] // 2] * len(self.positions)
            else:
                z_min = int(occupied[0])
                z_max = int(occupied[-1])
                z_values = [round(z_min + position * (z_max - z_min)) for position in self.positions]

            slices = torch.stack([volume[:, :, int(np.clip(z, 0, volume.shape[-1] - 1))] for z in z_values])
            slices = torch.nan_to_num(slices, nan=0.0, posinf=0.0, neginf=0.0)
            slices = F.interpolate(
                slices.unsqueeze(0),
                size=(self.image_size, self.image_size),
                mode="bilinear",
                align_corners=False,
            ).squeeze(0)
            d[key] = slices
        return d


class TinySliceEncoder(nn.Module):
    """A deliberately small 2D image encoder."""

    def __init__(self, embedding_dim: int) -> None:
        """Build a small CNN that keeps enough layout to memorize tiny sets."""
        super().__init__()
        self.features = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(3, 16, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
            nn.Conv2d(16, 32, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
            nn.Conv2d(32, 64, kernel_size=3, padding=1),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.MaxPool2d(2),
        )
        pooled_size = int(CONFIG["image_size"]) // 8
        self.projection = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(64 * pooled_size * pooled_size, int(CONFIG["encoder_hidden_dim"])),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(int(CONFIG["encoder_hidden_dim"]), embedding_dim),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Map a 3-slice image stack to an embedding."""
        return self.projection(self.features(x))


class SliceCLIP(nn.Module):
    """Encode query and target images into one shared embedding space."""

    def __init__(self, embedding_dim: int) -> None:
        """Create two tiny encoders and a fixed similarity scale."""
        super().__init__()
        self.query_encoder = TinySliceEncoder(embedding_dim)
        self.target_encoder = TinySliceEncoder(embedding_dim)
        self.similarity_scale = float(CONFIG["similarity_scale"])

    def forward(self, query: torch.Tensor, target: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Return all query-target similarities in the batch."""
        query_embedding = self.encode_query(query)
        target_embedding = self.encode_target(target)
        return self.similarity_scale * query_embedding @ target_embedding.T

    def encode_query(self, query: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Encode query images and normalize the embeddings."""
        return F.normalize(self.query_encoder(query), dim=1)

    def encode_target(self, target: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Encode target images and normalize the embeddings."""
        return F.normalize(self.target_encoder(target), dim=1)


class PairImageDataset(Dataset):
    """Training pairs backed by MONAI-cached slice stacks."""

    def __init__(self, pairs: list[dict[str, str | Path]], image_dataset: PersistentDataset) -> None:
        """Keep only positive pairs; each batch supplies in-batch negatives."""
        self.image_dataset = image_dataset
        self.id_to_index = {row["id"]: index for index, row in enumerate(image_dataset.data)}
        self.examples = [(str(pair["query_id"]), str(pair["target_id"])) for pair in pairs]

    def __len__(self) -> int:
        """Return the number of positive pair examples."""
        return len(self.examples)

    def __getitem__(self, index: int) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
        """Fetch a query-target image pair; training noise is sampled here."""
        query_id, target_id = self.examples[index]
        query_image = self._image(query_id)
        target_image = self._image(target_id)
        return query_image, target_image

    def _image(self, image_id: str) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Load one cached slice stack by image ID."""
        item = self.image_dataset[self.id_to_index[image_id]]
        return torch.as_tensor(item["image"]).float()


def read_csv(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    """Read a CSV into plain dictionaries."""
    with path.open(newline="") as f:
        return list(csv.DictReader(f))


def resolve_image_path(data_root: Path, image_path: str) -> Path:
    """Resolve manifest image paths relative to the dataset root."""
    path = Path(image_path)
    resolved = path if path.is_absolute() else data_root / path
    if resolved.exists():
        return resolved
    # The manifests list .nii.gz, but the released volumes are uncompressed .nii
    # (or vice versa); fall back to whichever extension exists on disk.
    text = str(resolved)
    alternate = Path(text[:-3]) if text.endswith(".nii.gz") else Path(text + ".gz")
    return alternate if alternate.exists() else resolved


def load_manifest_images(data_root: Path, csv_paths: list[Path], id_column: str, image_column: str) -> dict[str, Path]:
    """Load image IDs and paths from one or more manifest CSVs."""
    images: dict[str, Path] = {}
    for csv_path in csv_paths:
        for row in read_csv(csv_path):
            images[row[id_column]] = resolve_image_path(data_root, row[image_column])
    return images


def load_training_pairs(
    data_root: Path,
    train_pair_csvs: list[Path],
    train_query_csvs: list[Path],
    train_gallery_csvs: list[Path],
    train_label_csvs: list[Path],
) -> list[dict[str, str | Path]]:
    """Collect positive training pairs from pair CSVs or labelled pools."""
    pairs: list[dict[str, str | Path]] = []
    for csv_path in train_pair_csvs:
        for row in read_csv(csv_path):
            pairs.append(
                {
                    "query_id": row["query_id"],
                    "target_id": row["target_id"],
                    "query_path": resolve_image_path(data_root, row["query_image"]),
                    "target_path": resolve_image_path(data_root, row["target_image"]),
                }
            )

    query_images = load_manifest_images(data_root, train_query_csvs, "query_id", "query_image")
    target_images = load_manifest_images(data_root, train_gallery_csvs, "target_id", "target_image")
    for csv_path in train_label_csvs:
        for row in read_csv(csv_path):
            query_id = row["query_id"]
            target_id = row["target_id"]
            if query_id not in query_images or target_id not in target_images:
                continue
            pairs.append(
                {
                    "query_id": query_id,
                    "target_id": target_id,
                    "query_path": query_images[query_id],
                    "target_path": target_images[target_id],
                }
            )

    if not pairs:
        raise ValueError("No training pairs found. Pass --train-pair-csv or query/gallery/label CSVs.")
    return pairs


def collect_prediction_sets(
    data_root: Path,
    query_csvs: list[Path],
    gallery_csvs: list[Path],
) -> list[dict[str, dict[str, Path]]]:
    """Pair up query and gallery manifests for ranking."""
    if len(query_csvs) != len(gallery_csvs):
        raise ValueError("--query-csv and --gallery-csv must be passed the same number of times")

    sets = []
    for query_csv, gallery_csv in zip(query_csvs, gallery_csvs):
        queries = load_manifest_images(data_root, [query_csv], "query_id", "query_image")
        targets = load_manifest_images(data_root, [gallery_csv], "target_id", "target_image")
        sets.append({"queries": queries, "targets": targets})
    return sets


def monai_transform(augment: bool) -> Compose:
    """Build the MONAI pipeline; optional noise happens after cached slices."""
    deterministic = [
        LoadImaged(keys="image", image_only=True),
        EnsureChannelFirstd(keys="image"),
        Orientationd(keys="image", axcodes="RAS", labels=None),
        Spacingd(keys="image", pixdim=CONFIG["spacing_mm"], mode="bilinear"),
        ScaleIntensityd(keys="image", minv=0.0, maxv=1.0),
        SliceStackd(
            keys="image",
            positions=tuple(CONFIG["slice_positions"]),
            image_size=int(CONFIG["image_size"]),
        ),
        EnsureTyped(keys="image"),
    ] # monai persistent dataset knows which transforms are deterministic and caches the results after the last deterministic transform automatically
    random_augmentation = []
    if augment:
        random_augmentation.append(
            RandGaussianNoised(
                keys="image",
                prob=float(CONFIG["noise_probability"]),
                mean=0.0,
                std=float(CONFIG["noise_std"]),
            )
        )
    return Compose(deterministic + random_augmentation)


def make_image_dataset(images: dict[str, Path], example_root: Path, augment: bool) -> PersistentDataset:
    """Create the MONAI dataset; it handles cache reads/writes itself."""
    cache_dir = example_root / str(CONFIG["cache_dir"])
    cache_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    rows = [{"image": str(path), "id": image_id} for image_id, path in sorted(images.items())]
    return PersistentDataset(data=rows, transform=monai_transform(augment=augment), cache_dir=cache_dir)


def train_model(train_dataset: PairImageDataset) -> SliceCLIP:
    """Train the tiny dual encoder with in-batch negatives."""
    torch.manual_seed(int(CONFIG["seed"]))
    device = torch.device(str(CONFIG["device"]))
    model = SliceCLIP(int(CONFIG["embedding_dim"])).to(device)
    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=float(CONFIG["learning_rate"]))
    loss_fn = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    loader = DataLoader(
        train_dataset,
        batch_size=int(CONFIG["batch_size"]),
        shuffle=True,
        num_workers=int(CONFIG["num_workers"]),
    )

    model.train()
    for epoch in range(1, int(CONFIG["epochs"]) + 1):
        total_loss = 0.0
        total_seen = 0
        for query_batch, target_batch in loader:
            query_batch = query_batch.to(device)
            target_batch = target_batch.to(device)
            labels = torch.arange(len(query_batch), device=device)
            optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
            logits = model(query_batch, target_batch)
            loss = (loss_fn(logits, labels) + loss_fn(logits.T, labels)) / 2
            loss.backward()
            nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), max_norm=float(CONFIG["max_grad_norm"]))
            optimizer.step()
            total_loss += float(loss.item()) * len(query_batch)
            total_seen += len(query_batch)
        print(f"epoch {epoch:03d} loss={total_loss / max(total_seen, 1):.5f}")
    return model


@torch.no_grad()
def embed_images(model: SliceCLIP, image_dataset: PersistentDataset, encoder: str) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
    """Run one encoder once for each deterministic query/gallery image."""
    device = next(model.parameters()).device
    loader = DataLoader(image_dataset, batch_size=int(CONFIG["batch_size"]), shuffle=False)
    embeddings: dict[str, np.ndarray] = {}
    model.eval()
    for batch in tqdm(loader, desc="Embedding images"):
        image_batch = torch.as_tensor(batch["image"]).float().to(device)
        if encoder == "query":
            embedding_batch = model.encode_query(image_batch).detach().cpu().numpy().astype(np.float32)
        elif encoder == "target":
            embedding_batch = model.encode_target(image_batch).detach().cpu().numpy().astype(np.float32)
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown encoder: {encoder}")
        for image_id, embedding in zip(batch["id"], embedding_batch):
            embeddings[str(image_id)] = embedding
    return embeddings


@torch.no_grad()
def rank_targets(
    model: SliceCLIP,
    query_embeddings: dict[str, np.ndarray],
    target_embeddings: dict[str, np.ndarray],
) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    """Score every query against every target and return rankings."""
    device = next(model.parameters()).device
    model.eval()

"""Assignment-based rerankers (Sinkhorn, Hungarian) on top of the SliceCLIP baseline.

Retrains the same model as slice_clip_baseline.py (same seed / hyperparams), then
for every (dataset, split) query/gallery pool:
  1. builds the full query x target cosine-similarity matrix,
  2. produces three rankings:
       - argmax    : sort each row by raw similarity (parity with baseline)
       - sinkhorn  : exp(tau*S) -> doubly-stochastic via Sinkhorn -> sort
       - hungarian : linear assignment, assigned target first, then descending sim
  3. writes <method>_submission.csv plus a sim-matrix .npz cache.

Diagnostics:
  - Top-1 conflict rate per pool (how often the same target wins multiple queries
    under argmax). High = strong signal that bijection-aware rerankers will help.
  - Labelled MRR on the training set (biased; near 1.0 expected after 500 epochs
    of fit, so mainly a sanity check that rerankers don't break ties wrongly).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import csv
import json
import random
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import torch
from scipy.optimize import linear_sum_assignment

from slice_clip_baseline import (
    CONFIG,
    PairImageDataset,
    SliceCLIP,
    collect_prediction_sets,
    embed_images,
    load_training_pairs,
    make_image_dataset,
    train_model,
)


def cosine_sim(query_emb: dict[str, np.ndarray], target_emb: dict[str, np.ndarray]):
    qids = sorted(query_emb)
    tids = sorted(target_emb)
    Q = np.stack([query_emb[q] for q in qids]).astype(np.float32)
    T = np.stack([target_emb[t] for t in tids]).astype(np.float32)
    return Q @ T.T, qids, tids


def logsumexp(x: np.ndarray, axis: int) -> np.ndarray:
    m = x.max(axis=axis, keepdims=True)
    return (m + np.log(np.exp(x - m).sum(axis=axis, keepdims=True))).squeeze(axis)


def sinkhorn_log(S: np.ndarray, tau: float, n_iter: int) -> np.ndarray:
    """Log-domain Sinkhorn on exp(tau*S). Returns log-post matrix."""
    logK = tau * S
    logu = np.zeros(S.shape[0], dtype=np.float64)
    logv = np.zeros(S.shape[1], dtype=np.float64)
    for _ in range(n_iter):
        logu = -logsumexp(logK + logv[None, :], axis=1)
        logv = -logsumexp(logK + logu[:, None], axis=0)
    return logu[:, None] + logK + logv[None, :]


def rank_argmax(S: np.ndarray, qids, tids):
    return [
        {"query_id": qids[i], "target_id_ranking": " ".join(tids[j] for j in np.argsort(-S[i]))}
        for i in range(len(qids))
    ]


def rank_sinkhorn(S: np.ndarray, qids, tids, tau: float, n_iter: int):
    return rank_argmax(sinkhorn_log(S, tau, n_iter), qids, tids)


def rank_hungarian(S: np.ndarray, qids, tids):
    row_idx, col_idx = linear_sum_assignment(-S)
    assigned = {int(r): int(c) for r, c in zip(row_idx, col_idx)}
    rows = []
    for i in range(len(qids)):
        order = list(np.argsort(-S[i]))
        if i in assigned:
            t_star = assigned[i]
            order = [t_star] + [j for j in order if j != t_star]
        rows.append({"query_id": qids[i], "target_id_ranking": " ".join(tids[j] for j in order)})
    return rows


def mrr(rankings, gold: dict[str, str]) -> float:
    rr = []
    for row in rankings:
        if row["query_id"] not in gold:
            continue
        ranking = row["target_id_ranking"].split()
        try:
            rr.append(1.0 / (ranking.index(gold[row["query_id"]]) + 1))
        except ValueError:
            rr.append(0.0)
    return float(np.mean(rr)) if rr else 0.0


def top1_conflict_rate(S: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """Fraction of queries whose top-1 target is also top-1 for another query."""
    top1 = np.argmax(S, axis=1)
    _, counts = np.unique(top1, return_counts=True)
    conflicting = int((counts > 1).sum())  # gallery items that win multiple
    queries_in_conflict = int(np.sum([counts[np.where(np.unique(top1) == t)[0][0]] for t in top1 if counts[np.where(np.unique(top1) == t)[0][0]] > 1])) if conflicting else 0
    return queries_in_conflict / len(top1)


def write_submission(path: Path, rows):
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with path.open("w", newline="") as f:
        w = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=["query_id", "target_id_ranking"])
        w.writeheader()
        w.writerows(rows)


def parse_args():
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    p.add_argument("--data-root", type=Path, required=True)
    p.add_argument("--train-pair-csv", type=Path, action="append", default=[])
    p.add_argument("--query-csv", type=Path, action="append", required=True)
    p.add_argument("--gallery-csv", type=Path, action="append", required=True)
    p.add_argument("--out-dir", type=Path, default=Path("rerank_out"))
    p.add_argument("--ckpt", type=Path, default=Path("rerank_out/model.pt"))
    p.add_argument("--sim-cache", type=Path, default=Path("rerank_out/similarities.npz"))
    p.add_argument("--skip-train", action="store_true")
    p.add_argument("--sinkhorn-tau", type=float, default=10.0)
    p.add_argument("--sinkhorn-iter", type=int, default=50)
    return p.parse_args()


def main():
    args = parse_args()
    args.out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    args.ckpt.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    random.seed(int(CONFIG["seed"]))
    np.random.seed(int(CONFIG["seed"]))

    example_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
    data_root = args.data_root.resolve()

    train_pairs = load_training_pairs(data_root, args.train_pair_csv, [], [], [])
    prediction_sets = collect_prediction_sets(data_root, args.query_csv, args.gallery_csv)

    train_images: dict[str, Path] = {}
    for pair in train_pairs:
        train_images[str(pair["query_id"])] = Path(pair["query_path"])
        train_images[str(pair["target_id"])] = Path(pair["target_path"])

    inference_images: dict[str, Path] = {}
    for ps in prediction_sets:
        inference_images.update(ps["queries"])
        inference_images.update(ps["targets"])
    # also embed train images so we can compute labelled-train MRR diagnostics
    inference_images.update(train_images)

    print(json.dumps({
        "config": CONFIG,
        "num_train_images": len(train_images),
        "num_inference_images": len(inference_images),
        "num_train_pairs": len(train_pairs),
        "num_prediction_sets": len(prediction_sets),
    }, indent=2))

    train_image_dataset = make_image_dataset(train_images, example_root, augment=True)
    inference_image_dataset = make_image_dataset(inference_images, example_root, augment=False)
    train_dataset = PairImageDataset(train_pairs, train_image_dataset)

    device = torch.device(str(CONFIG["device"]))
    if args.skip_train and args.ckpt.exists():
        print(f"Loading model from {args.ckpt}")
        model = SliceCLIP(int(CONFIG["embedding_dim"])).to(device)
        model.load_state_dict(torch.load(args.ckpt, map_location=device))
    else:
        print("Training model (this matches the existing 0.37659 baseline)...")
        model = train_model(train_dataset)
        torch.save(model.state_dict(), args.ckpt)
        print(f"Saved checkpoint -> {args.ckpt}")

    print("\nEmbedding all images...")
    q_emb_all = embed_images(model, inference_image_dataset, encoder="query")
    t_emb_all = embed_images(model, inference_image_dataset, encoder="target")

    # --- Diagnostic: labelled MRR on training pairs (biased) ---
    train_qids = sorted({str(p["query_id"]) for p in train_pairs})
    train_tids = sorted({str(p["target_id"]) for p in train_pairs})
    gold = {str(p["query_id"]): str(p["target_id"]) for p in train_pairs}
    S_tr, qids_tr, tids_tr = cosine_sim(
        {q: q_emb_all[q] for q in train_qids},
        {t: t_emb_all[t] for t in train_tids},
    )
    print(f"\n=== Labelled-train diagnostic (queries={len(qids_tr)}, gallery={len(tids_tr)}; biased) ===")
    print(f"top-1 conflict rate (argmax): {top1_conflict_rate(S_tr):.3f}")
    print(f"  argmax   MRR={mrr(rank_argmax(S_tr, qids_tr, tids_tr), gold):.4f}")
    print(f"  sinkhorn MRR={mrr(rank_sinkhorn(S_tr, qids_tr, tids_tr, args.sinkhorn_tau, args.sinkhorn_iter), gold):.4f}")
    print(f"  hungari. MRR={mrr(rank_hungarian(S_tr, qids_tr, tids_tr), gold):.4f}")

    # --- Per-pool submissions + conflict diagnostics ---
    methods = {"argmax": [], "sinkhorn": [], "hungarian": []}
    sim_cache: dict[str, np.ndarray] = {}
    print("\n=== Per-pool conflict + ranking ===")
    for idx, ps in enumerate(prediction_sets):
        q_emb = {qid: q_emb_all[qid] for qid in ps["queries"]}
        t_emb = {tid: t_emb_all[tid] for tid in ps["targets"]}
        S, qids, tids = cosine_sim(q_emb, t_emb)
        sim_cache[f"S_{idx}"] = S
        sim_cache[f"qids_{idx}"] = np.array(qids)
        sim_cache[f"tids_{idx}"] = np.array(tids)
        print(f"  pool {idx}: Q={len(qids)} G={len(tids)} top-1 conflict rate={top1_conflict_rate(S):.3f}")
        methods["argmax"].extend(rank_argmax(S, qids, tids))
        methods["sinkhorn"].extend(rank_sinkhorn(S, qids, tids, args.sinkhorn_tau, args.sinkhorn_iter))
        methods["hungarian"].extend(rank_hungarian(S, qids, tids))

    np.savez(args.sim_cache, **sim_cache)
    print(f"\nSaved similarity cache -> {args.sim_cache}")

    for name, rows in methods.items():
        out = args.out_dir / f"{name}_submission.csv"
        write_submission(out, rows)
        print(f"  {name}: {len(rows)} rows -> {out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
